Assembly: The assembly fSprSpr1.1 is based on 62x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly ilHipFidi1.hap1.1 is based on 46x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements~(https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4). Order and orientation of scaffolds on Chromosome 23 from ~9.45-9.90Mb, and Chromosome B1 from 0-8.81Mb is unsure. Hap1 contains a representative version of a supernumerary B Chromosome (Chromosome B1), based on read coverage this likely exists in multiple copies in this assembly but it was not possible to seperate out all copies.

Assembly: The assembly ilOleArcu1.hap2.1 is based on 61x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. Chromosomes Z and W were identified by copy number in the diploid assembly. The order and orientation of scaffolds making up the W chromosomes is uncertain. Regions on chromosomes B1 (9.9 Mbp - 21.3 Mbp, 26.0 Mbp - 40.0 Mbp) and B2 (full length) are collapsed based on coverage, that suggests the potential presence of additional copies of these chromosomes. Chromosomes B1 B2 and B3 lack of ancestral BUSCO genes and have no alignment to closest relative genomes. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 19 (0 Mbp - 0.6 Mbp) and B1 (28.2 Mbp - 31.3 Mbp) are unknown.

Assembly: The assembly of Illex coindetii (xcIllCoin1) is based on 40X PacBio data and Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr).The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 24 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 5.3 Mb (with the largest being 3.9Mb). Additionally, 1,395 regions totaling 286 Mb (with the largest being 2.7 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using ptgaul. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 4 haplotypic regions were removed,totaling 3.9 Mb (with the largest being 1.6 Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size. The chromosome X has been assigned thanks to its coverage in PacBio reads divided by 2.

Assembly: The assembly ilMelLudo1.1 is based on 114x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. A manually phased assembly (based on HiC signal) was generated from a jointly curated primary and alt assembly. Both haplotypes are assembled to chromosome scale. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosomes in the alt assembly are named after their homolog in the primary assembly. This genome has a complex structure with a number of chromosomes forming multivalent structures. As a result there are a number of different possible formations of the different fused sections of chromosomes. This assembly represents one version. The alternate haplotype (Haplotype 2) represents the second haplotype reflecting the structure of the primary (Haplotype 1). The chromosomes in each haplotype are named in size order and do not reflect each other.

Assembly: The assembly drLabAnag1.hap1.1 is based on 36x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. A haplotypic inversion was observed in the region on chromosome 1 ( 50.2 - 53.5 Mbp).

Assembly: The assembly ilArcPodn1.1 is based on 27x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. A manually phased assembly (based on HiC signal) was generated from a jointly curated primary and alt assembly. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Assembly: The assembly xbLimFort5.1 is based on PacBio HiFi (23x coverage), 10X Genomics Chromium (44x coverage) and Hi-C Arima2 (81x coverage) data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/science/programmes/tree-of-life). The contigs were assembled with HiFiasm (version 0.16.1) in Hi-C integrated mode. Polishing was done with Long Ranger (version 2.2.2) mapper followed by Freebayes (version 1.3.1) variant caller. YaHS (version 1.0) was used for scaffolding. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Genome contains a number of heterozygous inversions due to divergent haplotypes.

Assembly: The assembly dcPolAvic1.1 is based on 68x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly ilCneLong2.hap1.1 is based on 65x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The order and orientation on Chromosome W from 6.98 to 27.76 Mb is uncertain. We observed a fusion between one pair of autosome chromosomes and the canonical W, which resulted in a large and fused W chromosome and a neo-sex chromosome named as Z2. One potential W2 chromosome was also detected.

Assembly: The assembly of Atherina boyeri (fAthBoy1) is based on 64X PacBio data and 144X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr).The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 1 contig weas identified as contaminants (bacterial), totaling 28 Kb. Additionally, 234 regions totaling 29.5 Mb (with the largest being 2.7 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using oatk. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 5 haplotypic regions were removed, totaling 5.1 Mb (with the largest being 1.5 Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly mPhyCat2.hap1.1 is based on 52x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds are named by synteny based on Physeter catodon (sperm whale) GCA_002837175.5. The chromosomes were named and oriented based on the reference Physeter catodon assembly GCA_002837175.5.The sex chromosomes were identified by read coverage and copy number in the haploid assembly. The exact order and orientation of the contigs in the centromers of chromosome 4 (66.3-73.7Mbp), chromsome 8 (40.0-53.7Mbp), chromosome 11 (135.4-149.9Mbp), chromosome 1 (37.7-41.9Mbp), chromosome 7 (55.6-60.5Mbp), chromosome 20 (38.1-54.6Mbp), chromosome 16 (61.5-68.3Mbp), chromosome 5 (81.5-88.6Mbp), chromosome 12 (67.1-81.7Mbp), chromosome 14 (31.8-36.3Mbp) and chromosome 17 (23.0-35.2Mbp) is unknown. A chromosome fusion of the reference chromosome 3 and 4 was observed. The new chromosome 3 originates from a fission of the reference chromosome 14.
